scenario: An agent can search, describe, and expand Codex sessions from indexed safe summaries while preserving plans, finals, touched files, tool metadata, and honest omitted markers.
expected_public_safe_evidence: fixture or bounded local session count
query string with no private transcript text
visible-to-indexed map confidence and ambiguity markers
app-server thread source coverage without raw turns
pass_criteria: Known plan and final-message fixture queries return the expected codex_thread refs.
Describe output identifies status and source refs without raw transcript dumps.
1k and 4k expansion profiles retain metadata, plans, finals, touched files, and safe summaries.
Skipped, truncated, or omitted data is named explicitly.
Visible title matches correlate to indexed session refs only when unique, while duplicate titles remain ambiguous and low confidence.
App-server thread signals improve freshness/source coverage without exposing preview, cwd, path, or turns.
fail_criteria: Search returns unrelated source families as successful Codex-thread evidence.
Expansion claims complete recall while limits skipped or omitted data.
Evidence includes raw transcript spans, local DB files, or secret-like strings.
Visible map reports ambiguous title collisions as high-confidence session matches.
known_gaps: Large private-store retrieval quality must be scored from public-safe summaries only.
Semantic relevance thresholds should be calibrated after more redacted fixtures exist.
Visible-map quality currently uses deterministic title/ref heuristics, not a learned semantic matcher.
next_action: Run fixture tests and #260 visible-map tests first, then bounded local smoke with public-safe summary and source-coverage evidence.
proof_boundary: This is a local public-safe beta scorecard example; it proves the review shape, not current retrieval or visible-map quality.
